model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Virginia holds a slight edge in efficiency metrics, ranking 13th in KenPom and 14th in Torvik with a top-15 defense that should disrupt Tennessee's offense, which has shown inconsistency with a 22-11 record and recent injury concerns for freshman forward Nate Ament. While Tennessee's defense is elite, their offensive efficiency is lower (35th in KenPom) and they lack the same depth as the Cavaliers, who boast a 29-4 record and superior rim protection. The underdog's path for Tennessee relies on hot three-point shooting and forcing turnovers, but Virginia's disciplined style, experience in the ACC, and recent form make them the more reliable pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Investigate Team Health, Recent Form, and Matchup Dynamics
reasoningStep.summary: I checked ratings, injury reports, and recent performance for both teams to assess their readiness and matchup advantages.
reasoningStep.evidence: Ratings show Virginia (KenPom 13, Torvik 14) and Tennessee (KenPom 15, Torvik 15) are closely matched, but Virginia has a better record (29-4 vs. 22-11). Tennessee's freshman forward Nate Ament is returning from a high-ankle sprain with a minutes restriction, per the Tennessean (March 12, 2026), which could limit their frontcourt depth. Virginia's defense (adjde 95.7) is slightly better than Tennessee's (adjde 95.0), and their experience in the ACC provides a tournament-ready edge.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Nate Ament injury update, Tennessee basketball forward available vs Auburn Skip to main content Home Sports Titans Predators Nashville SC Vanderbilt UT High Schools Other Colleges Sports Columnist Joe Rexrode NFL Draft Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA Nate Ament injury update, Tennessee basketball forward available vs Auburn Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel NASHVILLE - Freshman forward Nate Ament will play for Tennessee basketball in the SEC Tournament, according to a UT source. Ament, coming off a right high ankle sprain, will be on a minutes restriction. The 6-foot-10 freshman has missed Tennessee's last two games. He will be available in an undetermined role on March 12 as the No. 5 seed Vols (21-10) take on No. 12 Auburn (17-15) in the second round of the SEC Tournament at Bridgestone Arena (3 p.m. ET, SEC Network). modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Round: Round of 32

Prior committed picks: 50
